message:
Move documentation hook to command configuration

The synchronization hook moves from global hooks/hooks.json to the
/docs:write command's frontmatter. This scopes the hook to run only
when writing documentation, reducing unnecessary synchronization
checks on other file operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+title: Command-scoped documentation synchronization hook
+type: change
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2026-01-17T10:28:48.008383Z
+---
+
+The documentation synchronization hook now runs only during the `/docs:write` command instead of on every file operation. The hook configuration moved from `hooks/hooks.json` to the command's frontmatter, making the behavior more explicit and reducing unnecessary synchronization checks.

@@ -5,6 +5,12 @@ args:
   topic:
     description: What to document (defaults to uncommitted changes or last commit)
     required: false
+hooks:
+  PreToolUse:
+    - matcher: "Read|Edit|Write"
+      hooks:
+        - type: command
+          command: "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/synchronize-docs.sh"
 ---
 
 # Write documentation
